column3: Alkaloids are natural compounds with diverse chemical structures found in yarrow. They can have various effects on the body.
Tannins are polyphenolic compounds that give yarrow its astringent properties.
Yarrow contains flavonoids, which are antioxidants known for their potential health-promoting effects.
Sesquiterpene lactones are a class of compounds found in yarrow, which can have various biological activities.
Yarrow volatile oils contain aromatic compounds that contribute to its characteristic fragrance.
Coumarins are natural compounds present in yarrow that have a range of potential effects on the body.
Polyacetylenes are organic compounds found in yarrow, which can have distinct properties and bioactivity.
Yarrow contains glycosides, which are sugar-bound compounds with potential physiological effects.
Phytosterols in yarrow are plant-derived compounds that have been studied for their potential health benefits.
Yarrow may contain essential vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C, vitamin K, and potassium, which can play various roles in supporting health.
